neighborhoods.name: Southside / Five Points South
neighborhoods.description: Walkable entertainment district near UAB with restaurants, bars, and the landmark Storyteller fountain
neighborhoods.character: Walkable entertainment, UAB adjacent, dining scene
neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Birmingham. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: Homewood / Mountain Brook
neighborhoods.description: Over-the-mountain suburbs with excellent schools, tree-lined streets, and vibrant local shopping
neighborhoods.character: Affluent over-the-mountain, excellent schools, local shops

housing.predominantType: Tudor houses, bungalows, and traditional homes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Birmingham's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Trussville / Irondale
neighborhoods.description: Eastern suburbs with affordable housing, good schools, and the Irondale Cafe (Whistle Stop)
neighborhoods.character: Affordable suburban, good schools, literary heritage
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: Vestavia Hills / Hoover
neighborhoods.description: Southern suburbs with top-rated schools, Riverchase Galleria, and family-oriented communities
neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
